intro: On the longest day of the year, the light began to run out. They left me chambers of code — and only my mind to hold back the dark.

beats.line: They counted the day's losses in minutes of light. I learned to buy them back in letters.
Even the sun speaks in ones and zeros, if you are patient enough to listen.
Truth is just a lamp wired correctly. I am learning which switches to throw.
The days fold into one another. Knowing the pattern, I can already feel tomorrow.
He built a machine to think in his place, so that others might see the morning. Tonight, I am the machine.
The key turns. The horizon warms. Whatever the dark took, the light always returns — and so do we.

epilogue: Solstice Almanac — June has long been a month of returning light: of Pride and Juneteenth, of midsummer fires and the year's brightest dawn. This game is a small ode to Alan Turing, who broke codes to push back a darkness of his own, and whose dawn came far too late. Keep the light.
